revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本三大怨霊]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q30938239]]
Wikidata name: Three Great Onryō of Japan
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{字引|date=February 2018}}
'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!! Frugal!? The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|qq=|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by [[:en:Yomihon|yomihon]], [[:en:Kabuki|kabuki]], and other cultural influences during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

== Sugawara no Michizane ==
{{See|Sugawara no Michizane#Vengeful spirit legends and Kitano worship}}
{{See|{{ill|Seiryōden Lightning Strike Incident|ja|清涼殿落雷事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōden Lightning Strike Incident|qq=|1=Seiryōden lightning strike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561292}}}}
{{See|Tenjin faith}}
{{See|Tenjin (kami)}}

== Taira no Masakado ==
{{See|Taira no Masakado#Legend of the head}}
{{See|{{ill|Shōmonzuka|ja|将門塚|lt=Taira no Masakado's Grave|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonzuka|qq=|1=Shōmonzuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482436}}}}

== Emperor Sutoku ==
{{See|Emperor Sutoku#Vengeful spirit legends}}

== Notes ==

=== References ===
{{Reflist|2}}

== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Yūji Yamada|authorlink=Yūji Yamada|title=What Are Vengeful Spirits? Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku|publisher=[[Chūō Kōron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chūkō Shinsho]]|date=August 2014|isbn=978-4-12-102281-3|ref=harv}}
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]
* {{ill|Spirit (ghost)|ja|心霊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spirit (ghost)|qq=|1=Spirit (ghost)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490632}}
* [[:en:Ikiryō|Ikiryō]]
* [[:en:Posthumous name|Posthumous name]]
* {{ill|List of ghosts|en|List of ghosts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of ghosts|qq=}}

'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!! Frugal!? The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by [[:en:Yomihon|yomihon]], [[:en:Kabuki|kabuki]], and other cultural influences during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by [[:en:Yomihon|yomihon]], [[:en:Kabuki|kabuki]], and other cultural influences during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!! Frugal!? The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by [[:en:Yomihon|yomihon]], [[:en:Kabuki|kabuki]], and other cultural influences during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!! Frugal!? The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by [[:en:Yomihon|yomihon]], [[:en:Kabuki|kabuki]], and other cultural influences during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!! Frugal!? The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by [[:en:Yomihon|yomihon]], [[:en:Kabuki|kabuki]], and other cultural influences during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}.<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!! Frugal!? The Front and Back of Daimyo Processions: The Mysteries and Wonders of Sankin Kōtai|medium=E-book|language=Japanese|publisher=Jitsugyo no Nihon Sha}}</ref> Historian {{ill|Yūji Yamada|ja|山田雄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūji Yamada|1=Yūji Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223878}} argues that the perception of Sugawara no Michizane, Taira no Masakado, and Emperor Sutoku as the Three Great Vengeful Spirits was largely shaped by {{ill|Yomihon|en|Yomihon|WD=Q556900|lt=yomihon}}, {{ill|Kabuki|en|Kabuki|WD=Q199701|lt=kabuki}}, and other cultural influences during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.{{sfn|Yamada Yūji|2014|at=Preface}}

'''Japan's Three Great Vengeful Spirits''' (日本三大怨霊, Nihon Sandai Onryō) refers to three specific vengeful spirits in Japan: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}.<ref>{{cite video|和書|people=Hirofumi Yamamoto|date=2017-01-25|title=Super Fast!!
